description: Apktool: A critical Reverse Engineering utility designed natively for unpacking, decoding, modifying, and completely rebuilding 3rd party, closed, binary Android apps.

commands.explanation: Decodes the compressed raw Java/Smali bytecodes and unzips internal encrypted AndroidManifests leaving completely readable resources mapped natively in a new directory.
Surgically executes logic extraction completely avoiding disassembling dex files. The `.dex` files are left heavily raw avoiding enormous Smali generation times.
Inverts disassembly reading all heavily modified `.smali` codes bridging them automatically recreating absolute raw unaligned `.apk` payloads.
Injects localized OEM-specific graphical processing libraries allowing complex hardware-proprietary apps to disassemble cleanly.
Injects deep debug-mode parameters radically generating inherently step-compatible `.java` bridges for use natively in IDE architectures.
Forcefully drops new decoded folders instantly bypassing errors by actively eradicating previous analysis folders containing matching names.
Safely unlinks all locally stored OEM mapping libraries forcing the internal system framework paths to physically factory reset.
Directs Smali mapping exactly matching the canonical physical architecture array maintaining absolute raw 1:1 file paths during edits.
Overrides inherent package packaging algorithms explicitly leveraging advanced specified customized Android Asset Packaging Tools (AAPT2).
A critical system verification ensuring the installed internal library dependencies explicitly map to the proper java environment variable matrices.
